author: @HarleyPebley
text: Another trick for things like the s*x and bitcoin comments is to use the banned words filter in YT so you don't have to bother banning people, YT just automatically moves any comment with one of the listed words to the junk pile.

author: @vitordelima
text: There is a bias in any kind of moderation that can result in discreet censorship (in favor of certain interests) and stagnation, this is more common in other communities, but it still affects STEM a lot.

author: @vladimirroudnev7825
text: Great observation!  Moreover, the metaphor of gardening is actually very helpful when we try to develop a good policy - or evaluate an existing one -- for any complex system, such as scientific laboratory, technological startup, educational organization or even a nation.

author: @BiosWars

author: @PoorJohnn
text:
Robert I applaud your comments. I have a wide range of interests and have learned a lot watching your videos. I do get aggravated on other channels when I ask a simple question and the presenter never responds and I see I am not the only one  to express an interest. If they could see just how important it is to them.

Thank You for putting this out there.

author: @aspudkicker
text: Thats an interesting insight into the world of youtube.

author: @dejayrezme8617
text:
I've noticed this "conversation style spam" recently crop up too! They look innocent but their usernames invite you to go to their channel for the advertising. You report one but then you get another one to comment as a reply. This is why we can't have nice things haha.
We need massively improved social media with far more effort put into creating a positive influence on the world. There is a new study that looks interesting called "Stewardship of global collective behavior" on pnas. I only read the TLDR yet.

replies.author: @fritanke2318
replies.text: We, who read comments, can help out by using the report tool on those chat boots (bcoin, honey traps etc). Takes 10s and gives us a better environment. Atleast I hope it does.

replies.text: @fritanke2318 I hope too haha but you can't be sure. It might just be a placebo function

author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
Your humour, knowledge and down to earth attitude is great, you make this channel what it is.

Mrs Dingbatt doesn't get out much themselves these days

So the internet is useful,well,she's back in the Infirmary just now.

Nowt worrisome Except, I'm Stuck here with All Creatures Great n Small. 

(Bleeping Malingerers!)
Pigeons all over the place, and yon George is just waiting til I'm asleep, 

Nasty little vulture
he's plotting,definitely.
Last time he woke me up by sticking his beak in my left Ear!

(do you know any good  recipes?!)
Cheers for putting up with the Missus,,,

I love a lass with spirit, well that's the best reason I can think of in a pinch,Haha ha!
Thank you for the wise words,I've been learning loads here.

Much more than the practical things.
U have a good week,thanks  again .DD.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

replies.text:
​@ThinkingandTinkering Cheers,I will pass that on to her,she should be home soon,I hope!! 
She just took a fall,getting from the wheelchair to bed,and theyre just keeping an eye on her,n checking her over, theyll send her home soon,cos shes a terrible patient!!
If she sees owt wrong going on,shes quick to tell them,"If Id treated an animal like that,Id have lost my job!"!!  To be fair though,shes right,its one of the worst hospitals in the country.DD

author: @xspotbox4400
text:
Private channel is like being invited to a somebody's home, it's ethical for quests to respect hospitality of a host and behave accordingly, no arguing about that. Anarchy is based on general ethics, this is the only thing that separate it from total chaos.
But YouTube is also a corporate space, open for all kinds of people and characters, for a good reason. We live in a global society because of that, the very first time in human history. Let's not turn social media into social scoring, like China does to their own citizens, to weed out individuality. China is only one example, there are many military police and religious states out there, recruiting agents and snitches, fishing for free minded people, rebels and dissidents who they see as a treat to their regime, and it's eternal order.
In my opinion, we should all be aware of a human condition, but still allow people to grow, learn, get to know themselves better, change their ways and stop that god-damn trolling, spamming places with junk and engaging in pointless conversations just because they got up on a wrong foot or because their booze supply happened to run out.

Been following your creative content for over 20 years now, Sir,  i respect you for what you are, hope you never stop and i don't mind if this would be the last comment i published on your incredible science, metaphysics, technical philosophy and alchemy channel, you're truly one of a kind. I bother with comments sometimes because it fells good to be a part of your lore, like a pop icon of some sort, i don't think censorship can prevent people for being attracted to a magnitude of a very interesting information you demonstrated and published over decades. Your work is one of those things that will only gain importance over years to come, while all our comments will be long gone and forgotten, as we've never existed.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I don't censor mate - I. police. Any comment or dissenting opinion I keep - along as it isn't rude or abusive of others - that I do remove - not because of the ideas contained in it but the lack of respect for other and I don't do that straight away - my 'normal process' is to ask people first to be polite - if they refuse then they get banned - it seems a fair and balanced way to approach it to me - over 10 years with nearly a quarter of a million subs I have banned 50 people including the rash of sex sites I had about 2 years ago! not bad if you ask me lol

replies.author: @xspotbox4400
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering I understand Mr. Murray-Smith, don't worry about it, but sometimes those trolls can show genuine humor. I wrote this comment because of that guy last year, he was joking about energy conservation laws, i think. I know you had a dialog with that person, but as a reader, i never thought of you in relation to that dude. Probably thought he was having a blast on your channel, wonder how he feels about your work now, once he realized nobody is here for jokes or the theory, but we respect your time and effort invested in stuff hardly anybody in serious sciences pay attention to.

Those jokers seem so naive, they think science is only what is shown on a Discovery Channel and other popular media. He will come around, just like many others, wouldn't come here if they didn't find your experiments so exciting and refreshing. How can you prepare all those things and make a vlog almost every day is beyond my imagination, they don't make people like you no more, so thank you for your content and excuse me for my long posts.

author: @55Ramius

author: @scotttovey
text:
I came across a couple of gurus on the channels of preachers I listen too.

These two individuals were quite arrogant and operated from the point of view that they were more spiritual and had more knowledge than everyone on social media.

That point of view is not a very good place to be standing when God decides to have you tangle with someone that in fact, does know Him and His word.

I was quite cordial to them throughout the exchange and despite that fact, their last posts were not family friendly. 😏

That's the difference between one who thinks he has knowledge, and one who does in fact have knowledge.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: absolutely mate - I n my opinion gurus are not about God - gurus are about themselves - so, I am not surprised they were rude and arrogant - all the best and God bless

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering 😎👍

author: @drekpaprika
text:
This videos was way more eye opening to me then I expected. I am thinking about starting a channel and I really didn't put much thought into what kind of comments could appear if you leave the comment section open for all. Thanks for that. You changed my mind in less then 4 minutes. 
Also: I think of myself as a guru now. Cause I am one of those who always comments and argues with people about stuff. :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: wow - thank you mate - and a guru is one who has to be right arguing to help a person isn't being a guru - arguing because you have to be right is - I suspect your intent is only to help someone understand and for me that is just fine and doesn't make you a guru. Scott Tovey made a good post - a guru is usually easy to detect because they are often quite arrogant and to be honest - rude

replies.author: @drekpaprika
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Oh, ok. Then I am not a guru. I try not to be rude. Thanks man. You changed my mind again :)

replies.text:
It's awful,and the scammers as well, I follow an uploader, who had them all over.
Luckily, I'm a Mod there now,and I know little about technology, but my brother in law,can get through vpns to IP addresses, and was kind enough to help out,stopped them, with legality and technical know-how.
However, they seem to "Pop-up",in other places. .
YouTube has Many Wonderful people, like here, but also seems to attract lots of basement rats,who feel safe behind a keyboard, unfortunately...
They're just thieves and pests, and when I see them   trying to Steal from folks who don't have much themselves , it makes my blood boil, 
Woops,,,sorry I'll put the soapbox away,rant is over...Phew!!
Robert does a Sterling job keeping them away. A.Dingbatt.Mrs.
